steps.title: Identify the payment components
steps.content:
The problem tells us John's total cost breaks down into:
  • Down payment: $37 (paid upfront)
  • Monthly payments: p payments of $16 each = 16p
  • Total cost: \$165
The equation structure is: (down payment) + (monthly payments) = (total cost)

steps.title: Translate to an equation and match
steps.content:
Substituting our values into the equation structure:

$$37 + 16p = 165$$

Rearranging to standard form: $16p + 37 = 165$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
